origin: A snippet of Koine Greek biblical genealogy from the Gospel of Matthew (Matthew 1:13–14): “Azor begot Zadok; Zadok begot Achim; Achim begot Eliud,” forming part of the lineage tracing Jesus’ ancestry.
author: Most likely the evangelist traditionally associated with the text (Matthew), or more broadly the author/compiler of the Gospel of Matthew who preserved/translated this genealogical tradition into Greek.
thoughts: The author appears to be presenting a factual, record-like genealogy with little narrative emotion—likely motivated by demonstrating continuity of messianic/royal lineage (from David) and legitimizing Jesus’ heritage for a believing community.
audience: Early Jewish-Christian or Jewish readers familiar with genealogies and Old Testament expectations, and/or early Christian communities for whom establishing Jesus’ rightful ancestry was important.
written_when: Likely composed in the late 1st century CE (commonly dated to around ~80–90 CE, though some estimates place it closer to ~60–80 CE).
